problemsData.surface: I have no time for anything
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Sleep deprivation, caffeine overdose, downloading new productivity apps
problemsData.rightSolutions: Say no to a request this week without explanation; Turn off notifications for 2 hours; Write a 'not-to-do' list
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Rest is not a reward, but a necessity. | To let go of: The illusion of indispensability.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Aimless scrolling on social media, superficial chatting, escaping into binge-watching
problemsData.rightSolutions: Call someone on video instead of texting; Share an honest, imperfect story with a friend; Join an offline club/group
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Vulnerability is not weakness, but a bridge. | To let go of: The perfect mask.

problemsData.core: Lack of internal sense of worth
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Comparing yourself to others, hoarding degrees just for the paper, relentless hustle without rest
problemsData.rightSolutions: Write a list of 5 qualities that are not achievements; Celebrate a small success today; Unfollow 'successful' acquaintances who make you feel bad
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Your worth does not depend on your productivity. | To let go of: Constant comparison with others.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Over-insuring, constantly reading the news, checking on others excessively
problemsData.rightSolutions: Schedule 15 mins of 'worry time' daily, ban it otherwise; 5-4-3-2-1 grounding exercise; Write down the worst-case scenario, then tear it up
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Uncertainty is a part of life. | To let go of: The desire to control the future.

problemsData.surface: I can't find happiness in my relationship
problemsData.layer2: Expecting happiness from the other person
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Constant partner switching, the urge to change the other, sulking
problemsData.rightSolutions: Spend an evening alone with a hobby; List what you expect from the other and give it to yourself; Book couple's or individual therapy
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Emotional self-regulation. | To let go of: Waiting for a savior.

problemsData.layer4: Loss of connection with the authentic self
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Alcohol, locking yourself in a dark room, suppressing feelings
problemsData.rightSolutions: Seek professional help (psychiatrist/psychologist); 10 mins walk in sunlight daily; Cold shower; Journaling about feelings
problemsData.lesson: To learn: The power of small actions. | To let go of: 'I'll do it when I feel better'.

problemsData.layer4: Lack of safety, the body as armor
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Starvation, fad diets, body image obsession, self-hatred
problemsData.rightSolutions: Keep an emotion journal: 'What do I feel before eating?'; Put down the cutlery after every bite; Find other sources of joy (e.g. hot bath)
problemsData.lesson: To learn: To experience emotions, not eat them. | To let go of: Food as a comforting function.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Giving up goals, self-torment, procrastination in the name of perfection
problemsData.rightSolutions: Do something intentionally wrong (e.g. ugly drawing) and don't fix it; Use time-boxing for a task; Rewrite the inner critic's sentences
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Done is better than perfect. | To let go of: All-or-nothing thinking.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Playing the martyr, passive sulking, mistrusting everyone
problemsData.rightSolutions: Practice saying 'Let me think about it' instead of an immediate yes; Write down your rights on paper; Say: 'I don't have the capacity for this right now'
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Boundaries inspire respect. | To let go of: The fear of not being loved if you say no.

problemsData.core: The unbearableness of the present moment
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Flexing willpower without help, self-flagellation, replacing one addiction with another
problemsData.rightSolutions: Find an anonymous group or addictologist today; Delete tempting phone numbers/apps; Make an emergency plan for cravings
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Asking for help is strength. | To let go of: Shame and secrecy.

problemsData.layer2: The illusion of the perfect choice
problemsData.layer4: Memory of bad decisions or criticism
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Shifting the decision to others, endless pros and cons lists, delaying until the decision is moot
problemsData.rightSolutions: Flip a coin and observe your feelings at the result; Give yourself 2 mins for small decisions (e.g. at a restaurant); Take risks with small stakes
problemsData.lesson: To learn: There is no perfect decision, only a chosen path. | To let go of: Retrospective rumination (FOMO).

problemsData.layer4: Experience of betrayal in the past
problemsData.core: Lack of fundamental sense of security
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Isolation, aggressive demeanor, looking for ulterior motives behind every word
problemsData.rightSolutions: Do a reality check with a trusted friend ('I see it this way, is it realistic?'); Keep a gratitude journal of positive interactions; Seek professional help
problemsData.lesson: To learn: The world is not just black and white. | To let go of: The victim narrative.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Even more training, work, or partying to exhaustion, unjustified use of sleeping pills
problemsData.rightSolutions: Sit for 5 mins in silence without devices; Walk in nature without music/podcasts; Practice progressive muscle relaxation
problemsData.lesson: To learn: 'Being' is just as important as 'doing'. | To let go of: The belief that idleness is a sin.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Obsessively watching social media, belittling others, gossiping
problemsData.rightSolutions: Write down 3 things daily you are grateful for; Unfollow those who trigger envy; Turn envy into a goal: 'What can I do to have that too?'
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Another's success is not your failure. | To let go of: The lack-focus.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Forced positive affirmations in the mirror, series of plastic surgeries, self-punishment
problemsData.rightSolutions: Write a letter to yourself as if addressing a friend; Touch your chest when you have heavy feelings (self-compassion); Work with a pro on your inner critic
problemsData.lesson: To learn: You can be kind to yourself too. | To let go of: The voice of the inner tyrant.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Nodding to everything, passive-aggressive remarks, fleeing the room
problemsData.rightSolutions: Practice 'I-statements' ('It hurts when...'); Disagree on a low-stakes issue (e.g. what movie to watch); Stand your ground, don't walk away
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Conflict can clarify a relationship. | To let go of: The 'good kid' role.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Extreme sports without calculated risk, drugs, self-harm just to feel something
problemsData.rightSolutions: Seek body-oriented therapy; Hot-cold contrast shower; Touch textures around you; Name objects in the room out loud
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Being in the body is safe. | To let go of: The fear of pain.

problemsData.core: The body as a communication channel
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Hoarding symptom-suppressing drugs without investigating the cause, blaming the body, raging at the illness
problemsData.rightSolutions: Keep a symptom diary with emotional context; Learn relaxation techniques (autogenic training); Ask your symptom: 'What do you force me to do / save me from?'
problemsData.lesson: To learn: The body is your friend that signals. | To let go of: Treating the body as an object.

problemsData.layer3: Lack of acknowledgment of one's own pain
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Planning revenge, endlessly repeating the story to others, passive punishment
problemsData.rightSolutions: Write a letter to the offender, don't send it, burn/tear it; Acknowledge your own pain by crying/raging (in safety); Seek a pro for grief counseling
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Forgiveness is about your freedom. | To let go of: The desire for the past to change.

problemsData.layer2: Loss of initial momentum (lack of perseverance)
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Starting newer and newer projects, self-flagellation, seeking external enforcers
problemsData.rightSolutions: Break the task into micro-steps; Finish one tiny, long-overdue thing this week; Celebrate partial results
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Motivation follows action, not the other way around. | To let go of: Constantly chasing the magic of novelty.

problemsData.layer2: Chronic neglect of one's own needs
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Increasing coffee and energy drinks, 'I can just survive this week' attitude, alcohol
problemsData.rightSolutions: Go on vacation without your laptop; Start a hobby that has no 'utility'; Sleep 8 hours daily; Find a career counselor or therapist
problemsData.lesson: To learn: You are not a machine. | To let go of: The belief that rest must be earned.

problemsData.layer4: Early experience of abandonment or engulfment
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Ghosting, finding flaws in the other, escaping under the guise of freedom
problemsData.rightSolutions: Stay in the situation even if it's uncomfortable (a little longer); Talk about your fears to your partner; Therapy for attachment patterns
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Independence and attachment can coexist. | To let go of: Fleeing as the primary reaction.

problemsData.layer4: Negative beliefs about money or unworthiness
problemsData.core: Lack of abundance mindset and responsibility
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Taking out more loans, gambling, leaving bills unopened
problemsData.rightSolutions: Download an expense tracking app and log everything; Open all envelopes today; Set up automatic savings (even a tiny amount)
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Money is a tool, not an enemy. | To let go of: The desire for instant gratification.

problemsData.surface: Everyone is stupid, only I am smart
problemsData.layer3: Compensating for deep feelings of inferiority
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Lecturing others, cynical remarks, intellectual isolation
problemsData.rightSolutions: Listen to someone completely without interrupting or judging; Apologize if you were wrong; Find an environment where you are a beginner (learn something new)
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Humility is not humiliation. | To let go of: The need to always be right.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Renting storage units, buying organizer boxes instead of decluttering, 'it will be good for something later' mindset
problemsData.rightSolutions: Throw away/donate 3 items today; Take a photo of the memory before discarding the object; Practice non-attachment (mindfulness)
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Your memories are inside you, not in objects. | To let go of: Junk that provides false security.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Setting the clock forward, making excuses, rushing with a taxi
problemsData.rightSolutions: Leave 15 minutes early intentionally; Bring a book so waiting isn't an issue; Admit: 'I'm late, I'm sorry' (without excuses)
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Punctuality is respecting the other's time. | To let go of: Childish defiance.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Copying others, forced brainstorming, giving up on creating
problemsData.rightSolutions: Buy a cheap notebook and scribble in it ugly; Dance at home to music alone; Do something purely for the process, without an end goal
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Every human is a creative being. | To let go of: Performance anxiety in creation.

problemsData.surface: I can't talk to my family
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Screaming, total silence, expecting them to change on their own
problemsData.rightSolutions: Set your boundaries: 'I don't want to talk about this'; Try family constellation or therapy; Accept that you can't change them, only your attitude
problemsData.lesson: To learn: As an adult, you can redefine the relationship. | To let go of: The desire for the perfect family.

problemsData.surface: I don't know what I want
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Following others, going to a psychic instead of deciding, endless procrastination
problemsData.rightSolutions: Try a new activity every week; Write a list of what you DO NOT want; Spend time in silence to hear your own thoughts
problemsData.lesson: To learn: The answers are within you, not outside. | To let go of: Conformity.

problemsData.layer1: The child as a symptom bearer
problemsData.layer4: Need for attention or system stabilization
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Only treating the child medically, blaming the child, excessive worrying and overprotection
problemsData.rightSolutions: Address your own stress levels and relationship; Ask for family therapy consultation; Let the child sometimes 'just be', don't treat them as a patient
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Children reflect their environment. | To let go of: Guilt and excessive control.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Withdrawing at the last minute, downplaying achievements, hiding
problemsData.rightSolutions: Visualize success and the feelings that come with it; Share your achievement proudly with one person; Practice accepting compliments ('Thank you' without excuses)
problemsData.lesson: To learn: You have the right to abundance and success. | To let go of: False modesty.

problemsData.layer3: Fear that it won't be good enough
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Self-discipline through scolding, unrealistic deadlines, 'tomorrow I will do this all day' illusion
problemsData.rightSolutions: 5-minute rule: Just do it for 5 mins, then you can stop; Break the task into ridiculously small steps; Reward yourself for starting
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Action reduces anxiety. | To let go of: Waiting for perfect conditions.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Violent suppression of memories, alcohol, avoidant behavior
problemsData.rightSolutions: Seek trauma therapy (e.g. EMDR, Somatic Experiencing); Grounding exercises (touch 3 objects); Rewrite the end of your story on paper
problemsData.lesson: To learn: The past is gone, you can be safe in the present. | To let go of: Self-blame for what happened.

problemsData.layer2: Lack of internal judgment of worth
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Asking everyone ('Do you think this is good?'), hunting for likes, self-abandonment
problemsData.rightSolutions: Make a decision without asking anyone; Wear clothes you like, not what's fashionable; Write down your values
problemsData.lesson: To learn: You are living your life, not them. | To let go of: The desire for popularity.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Alcohol to relax, compulsive TV watching, collapsing from exhaustion
problemsData.rightSolutions: Practice long exhalations; Use a weighted blanket; Somatic therapy; Rocking or slow walking
problemsData.lesson: To learn: A sense of safety must be built in the body. | To let go of: Continuous control.

problemsData.surface: I am introverted, and that's a problem
problemsData.layer1: Labeling a personality trait as a flaw
problemsData.layer2: Compulsive pursuit of the extroverted ideal
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Forced social life, acting, guilt over solitude
problemsData.rightSolutions: Acknowledge your strengths (e.g. deep attention); Say no to big parties without guilt; Recharge alone with a book/walk
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Quiet strength is also strength. | To let go of: The pressure to conform to a noisy world.

problemsData.layer4: Lack of trust in the flow of life
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Thinking even more about the solution, night-time rumination, indecisiveness
problemsData.rightSolutions: Do something on the physical plane (dishwashing, running); Brain dump thoughts on paper so they don't circle your head; Meditation (focus on breath)
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Thoughts are not facts. | To let go of: Overthinking as a problem-solving strategy.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Compulsive search for 'useful' hobbies, learning languages instead of resting, envying others
problemsData.rightSolutions: Remember what you liked in childhood; Try something without stakes (e.g. coloring); Go to a trial class somewhere
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Joy itself is a goal. | To let go of: Utilitarianism.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Passive sulking, creating drama, abruptly cutting off relationships
problemsData.rightSolutions: Ask: 'How exactly did you mean that?'; Wait 24 hours before reacting; Realize: the other person's behavior is about them, not you
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Others' opinions are not your reality. | To let go of: Assumptions.

problemsData.core: Giving up the right to self-defense
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Swallowing it, then exploding; grumbling to yourself; avoidance
problemsData.rightSolutions: Practice saying 'No' in front of a mirror; Enroll in self-defense class or assertiveness training; Stand up straight in posture
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Your anger is a warning system that protects you. | To let go of: The fear of conflict.

problemsData.surface: They take advantage of my empathy
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Total isolation, cynicism, playing the martyr
problemsData.rightSolutions: Set a time limit for listening to complaints; Imagine a bubble around yourself; Say: 'I can't listen right now because I am tired'
problemsData.lesson: To learn: You can't help others if your cup is empty. | To let go of: The savior of the world role.

problemsData.surface: I can't let go of control
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Doing everything alone, constantly correcting others, checking things late into the night
problemsData.rightSolutions: Delegate a task and DO NOT check it; Trust someone with a small detail; Practice accepting 'good enough'
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Control is an illusion. | To let go of: Responsibility for everything and everyone.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Overacting roles, compulsive conformity in appearances, self-blame
problemsData.rightSolutions: Wear clothes/accessories that you like; Follow people who freely express their gender; Redefine what it means for you to be a man/woman
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Gender is a spectrum and an individual experience. | To let go of: Boxes and labels.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Jumping from one relationship to another, keeping the TV on for background noise, constantly calling people
problemsData.rightSolutions: Go to the movies or a restaurant alone; Spend a weekend with a 'digital detox' alone; Get to know yourself (journal, self-knowledge group)
problemsData.lesson: To learn: To be your own best company. | To let go of: Clinging to others.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: More lies to avoid exposure, pretending, believing your own lies
problemsData.rightSolutions: Admit a small lie to someone; Start practicing 'radical honesty' in small things; Seek a therapist to process shame
problemsData.lesson: To learn: The truth sets you free. | To let go of: The fear of being exposed.

problemsData.core: Rejection of one's own life rhythm
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Panic-driven midlife crisis decisions, sinking into depression, envying
problemsData.rightSolutions: List everything you have achieved so far (that is not a societal expectation); Formulate your own goals; Talk to older people about the curves in their life path
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Life is not a race. | To let go of: 'Should' thoughts.

problemsData.layer2: Pushing own needs to the background
problemsData.layer3: Feeling of worth only through serving others
problemsData.wrongSolutions: Giving unsolicited advice, taking on others' problems, getting offended if help is not accepted
problemsData.rightSolutions: Help only when asked (make it a rule); Ask: 'How can I help?' instead of just doing; Turn the focus on yourself: 'What do I need?'
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Everyone is responsible for their own life. | To let go of: Taking responsibility for others.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Complaining to everyone, passivity, waiting for miracles or apologies
problemsData.rightSolutions: Examine your own role in conflicts; Take responsibility for your feelings ('I feel this way'); Act instead of reacting
problemsData.lesson: To learn: You are the author of your life, not an extra. | To let go of: Blaming.

problemsData.wrongSolutions: Forced drastic changes that fail immediately; looking for excuses; procrastination
problemsData.rightSolutions: Change one tiny routine in the morning (e.g. different route, different mug); Acknowledge your fear; Ask for support (coach, friend)
problemsData.lesson: To learn: Change is the only constant. | To let go of: Clinging to the old self.
